persona: a freelance graphic designer juggling three client projects
topic: preparing for an upcoming interview or presentation
text: Okay so I'm sitting in my car at Whole Foods right now because apparently I can't get a decent signal at the office and I've got three client projects going and I'm supposed to be prepping for this interview with Johnson & Johnson tomorrow and I'm just so frustrated because I keep thinking I'm being efficient but I keep getting distracted and I just need to focus for five minutes but then my phone rings and it's Sarah from the startup and she wants to talk about her logo and I'm like, okay fine, I'll help but then I'm just going to have to reschedule my Johnson & Johnson prep and that's the third time this week and I'm starting to think that maybe I should just take a step back from the startup work because it's so chaotic and then I look at the clock and it's 11:47 and I've only been in this car for like ten minutes and I should probably head to the office but I can't because I'm going to have to deal with the printer issue that was reported yesterday and I'm just so tired of these constant interruptions um I really need to set some boundaries here and maybe start charging more for my time because honestly I'm working 14-hour days right now and I'm not getting any recognition for it which is really frustrating and I've been putting in so much effort with the Johnson & Johnson presentation I've got the slides done and everything but I'm still worried about the whole thing and I've been practicing my pitch and I'm just hoping it works out because I really need this job and I just want to be done with this whole chaos and I'm starting to think I'm going to need to call my mom and ask for advice because this is getting overwhelming and I'm just going to have to find some way to get some peace and quiet maybe I'll try calling the office and see if I can get some work done there instead um I just wish I could have like ten minutes to myself without anything interrupting me.

persona: a hobbyist woodworker with a garage workshop
topic: planning an event (a party, a wedding, a team offsite)
setting: right after the event they are talking about
text:
Oh man, I just finished talking with Sarah about the wedding party planning and I am so pumped about this. She's got this big event coming up at the end of next month, and I'm thinking this could be exactly what I need to finally get my workshop organized properly. Um, she said she wants something rustic, like a barn style affair, and she's already got a venue picked out at the old Riverside Farm that she's been looking at for like six months now.

I told her I could probably handle some of the woodwork for the decorations, and she was really excited about that. I mean, I've been working on a few projects in my garage for the last couple weeks, and I think I've finally found my rhythm. She mentioned something about maybe needing a couple of wooden display stands for the guest book and for some of the photos, and I was thinking that would be perfect for some of the stuff I've been planning to build.

The wedding is actually for her sister, so it's going to be really special. She said there are like twenty guests expected, and they want everything to look really authentic. I was thinking about making some of the signs out of reclaimed barn wood, or maybe I'll go with some of the pine I've been saving from my workshop. I've got this stack of planks that I've been planning to use for some kind of project, and this might be the perfect time to actually put them to use.

Sarah mentioned something about wanting the place to feel like it's been there for decades, and honestly, that's exactly what I was hoping to achieve with some of my work. She said they're going to have a big garden party, so the wooden elements should complement the outdoor setting. I'm thinking I could make some of the decorative pieces out of some of the maple that I've got stored in the back of my garage.

She told me that the actual wedding date is the twenty-seventh, so that gives me about three weeks to get everything done. I can work on some of it during the week, and I might need to put in a few extra hours on the weekend. I've got to be careful about not overcommitting myself though, because I still have the woodworking class I'm taking next month, and I don't want to let that slide.

But I'm really excited about this, because it's the first real commercial work I've been able to land, and I can feel my confidence building with every conversation I have with her. She mentioned she's already sent out some of the invitations, and they're going to be very traditional with the old-style stationery. I was thinking that maybe I could do some of the wood carving for some of the decorative elements, like maybe a few small wooden signs that will go around the guest area.

I think I'm going to start measuring everything out this week, so I can have an idea of how much material I'll need. I've got the basic measurements from Sarah, but I'll want to get the exact dimensions myself. I'm looking forward to seeing how all of it turns out, and I'm really grateful that she trusted me with this project. I've got a feeling this is going to be the start of something really exciting.

persona: a high-school chemistry teacher
topic: notes on an article they just read or a video they just watched
setting: right after the event they are talking about
text: Okay so I just watched this video about the periodic table and I'm still buzzing, you know what's wild, the guy from MIT Dr. Chen he was talking about how lithium was actually discovered in 1817 by Johann Arfvedson and I'm like wait that's literally the same year I was born right, anyway so he showed this diagram of the electron configuration and I was like okay this is the part where we talk about the 2-8-8-2 arrangement which is basically what we learned in freshman year but it's still so cool when you see it visualized, like the way he explained the ionization energy it's like the energy needed to remove an electron and it's measured in kilojoules per mole which is so much more precise than the old way we used to just estimate, I also liked how he talked about the difference between metallic and nonmetallic properties and how sodium's reactivity increases down the group, I'm thinking we should definitely watch this video next week and maybe do some hands-on activity with the actual periodic table, oh and he mentioned something about the new superheavy elements like oganesson which is the last element in the periodic table and it's so unstable that it only exists for microseconds but it's still fascinating, I'm definitely going to have to read up more on that, I'm so excited to share this with my students tomorrow they're going to love it, I mean who doesn't love a good chemistry video that actually makes sense, I think it's going to be a great way to start our new unit on atomic structure, anyway I'm going to make sure to save that link to the video, I should probably even print out a few notes, this is going to make such a difference in how we teach this topic I can already tell, I love how science is just always evolving you know, um, I wonder if I should send this to the department head, uh, yeah I'll do that, and I'm also going to check if there are any other resources online, uh, I think that's it for now, thanks for listening, I mean I'm just going to leave this here, um, okay, done.

persona: an IT consultant who travels to client sites most weeks
topic: brainstorm of ideas for a new project
setting: in the kitchen while doing something else
text:
Okay so I'm in the kitchen right now, trying to think through this new project proposal, which is driving me absolutely crazy, I mean like really driving me crazy. I've been working with Johnson Construction for like six months now, and they keep coming back with these vague requests, right? Yesterday I met with Sarah at 2 PM, and she was like "we need something that tracks equipment better," which is great, but she didn't even tell me what kind of equipment, or how many trucks they have, or even how many locations, I'm sorry, how many locations they actually have. 

I mean this is literally the third time this month they've come to me with these half-baked ideas, and I'm supposed to build a whole system around them, and then they wonder why it takes forever, right? I just spent twenty minutes on the phone with the hardware guy, Bob, who says they can get a barcode scanner setup for about six hundred bucks, but we'd need to order it in bulk because they're so cheap, I mean, they won't even pay for the delivery, that's just ridiculous, right? 

I think I'm going to write up this proposal tomorrow morning at the office, but I'm pretty sure I'm going to have to come back to them with a proper scope, I mean, I'm just going to have to come back to them with a proper scope, and I was thinking maybe something like a mobile app for field workers, but I need more concrete numbers before I can estimate the cost, like I really need more concrete numbers. 

Oh, and I need to call the network people today because we're supposed to be upgrading their whole system next month, but I don't think they've even told them what they want to upgrade, so I'll probably be on hold for an hour, God, I hate these meetings, I really do, I hate these meetings.

persona: an amateur runner training for their first marathon
topic: capturing a product or business idea before it slips away
text:
Hey, so I'm sitting here at like 11:45 PM, can't sleep, and I keep getting these thoughts about this idea I had today. Um, I was running my usual route, you know, the one that goes past the big parking lot where that new coffee place opened up, right on Maple Street, and I was thinking about this whole thing with the delivery app, but it's not really delivery app, it's like a whole different approach, you know?

So here's the thing, I had this moment of clarity at like 5:30 this afternoon, right after my 20-minute run, which is weird because I usually feel like I'm going to pass out or something, but this time I felt like actually I had something, I don't know, a breakthrough or whatever. Um, I was thinking about how all these people are struggling to get their groceries or packages or whatever, and then I was thinking about how there are these little businesses in neighborhoods that are just struggling, right? Like those local places that get it, that understand their community, but then they can't keep up with technology or something.

So I've been thinking about this for like a week now, and I keep thinking about this idea of like a community-based delivery network, but instead of like big corporations, it's more like, what if we had local people who are already in the neighborhood, like the guy who walks dogs or the woman who volunteers at the community center, they could help with this. Like, what if we made it so that every neighborhood had this kind of system where people who are already doing stuff in their community could get paid to help other people in their community with deliveries.

It's like a local Uber, but instead of just Uber drivers, it's like neighbors helping neighbors. I know it sounds crazy, but think about it, right? The whole concept is about making it easier for people to be helpful, and also for local businesses to stay open, and also for people who might be isolated or elderly to get help with shopping or things like that.

But I keep thinking about the logistics, like how do you verify people, how do you make sure it's safe, how do you make sure people are reliable, you know? And also, how do you make the system profitable? I've been thinking about the fact that I've been doing this running thing for about six months now, and I've been training for my first marathon, which is going to be in three weeks, so I'm still pretty new to all of this, but I'm thinking about how I could make this work.

I've been talking to this guy, I don't remember his name, but he's from the tech company on Fifth Street, and he said that the thing about this kind of idea is that it needs to be built on trust, right? It needs to be about people who know each other, you know? Like, that's the whole point of making it community-based, so you're not just another customer, you're a part of the system.

I just want to make sure I don't forget this idea, because I think it's actually really solid, and I don't want to lose track of it. I want to write it down tomorrow morning, and I'll probably make a little app or something, I don't know, but it's definitely something worth pursuing, I think.

I mean, I'm running this marathon because I want to prove to myself that I can do something really hard, but I also want to figure out if I can build something that matters, and I think this idea might be that thing. I'm going to have to get some sleep though, because I have to be up at 4:30 AM to get ready for the run tomorrow, so I'll keep thinking about it.

persona: a parent of two who does most of the household cooking
topic: errands and shopping they need to get done
text:
Okay so I need to think about what we need to get done this week. I'm going to grab the grocery list from the fridge and then I'll need to hit the store before it closes at ten. I think we're running low on milk and eggs and I need some fresh vegetables.

Actually I should probably check the pantry first, let me look at that real quick. Oh yeah, we've got some frozen corn and peas, and I think there's still some chicken broth in the freezer. That's good, I don't need to buy that right now. But I really need to get some ground beef, maybe like two pounds or so, and some fresh lettuce.

The store is on Fifth Street, that's where I usually go, the one with the parking lot that's not too crowded. I'll need to go there on Tuesday night, maybe around seven thirty, that's usually when it's not too busy. I don't want to go in there right after dinner, but I also don't want to wait too long because then I'll be rushing and I'll make mistakes.

I need to remember to pick up the cleaning supplies too, the paper towels and the dishwasher detergent. I know I'm almost out of that, and I don't want to have to make another trip. Actually let me check the pantry again, I think I might have some paper towels left.

Oh, and I need to get my laundry done too. The washing machine is broken again, I'll have to call the repair guy, he said he'd come by this week. I'll call him tonight, I have his number on my phone.

I'm going to need to get some more coffee beans, I'm running low. We've got like three bags left, so that's probably not enough for the whole week. I'll need to get a bigger bag, maybe the medium roast, the one we like.

I also need to go to the pharmacy for some vitamins. I know I got those last month, but I don't remember what they were. I need to check the bathroom cabinet, I'll look in the medicine cabinet.

Okay so I need to get the groceries, I need to get the laundry done, I need to call the repair guy, and I need to get some vitamins. That's a lot, but I'll be able to do it.

I also need to check on the kids, they're going to want to know about their dinner plans. I think we're having spaghetti tonight, but I should make sure I have the right amount of meat sauce. I'll need to buy some pasta noodles too, and I'll need to get some cheese.

I also need to get the dog food, I'm almost out of that too. I'll need to get the regular brand, I think the blue dog food, that's what they like.

I'm going to need to plan my meals for the week, I've got that chart in the kitchen, I'll look at that too.

I should probably also go to the post office, I need to mail that package to my mom. I think I have a stamp somewhere, but I'll need to check.

I'm starting to feel a bit overwhelmed, but I'll take it one thing at a time. I'll make a list of everything I need to do, that always helps. I need to get the groceries, I need to call the repair guy, I need to get the laundry done, I need to get the dog food, I need to get the vitamins, and I need to mail that package.

That's actually not too bad, I can do that. I'll just make sure to go to the store early in the morning, so I don't have to rush. I'll also make sure I have my list handy.

I don't want to forget anything important. I'll make sure to check the kids' homework, and I'll need to get some more snacks for the kids. I think they're running low on the crackers.

Okay, I think I've got everything I need to think about. I'm going to go get the list from the fridge, and then I'll start planning what to do first.

persona: an IT consultant who travels to client sites most weeks
topic: recap of a call with a customer or client
setting: in the kitchen while doing something else
text:
Hey so I just got back from the call with Johnson Construction and I'm still trying to process this whole thing um you know I'm supposed to be making a sandwich but I keep getting distracted by this weird noise from the kitchen anyway so they're still having issues with their network setup at the new office on Fifth Street

I told them that the problem with the WiFi wasn't actually the router like they thought it was um it was the firewall settings that were blocking their VPN connections they had been working with their old IT guy for like six months and he had never even looked at the firewall configuration

The guys name is Mark he's the project manager over there and he kept saying that the network just stopped working all of a sudden I'm like no Mark you know what it's because they moved their office and the IP addresses got changed I had to reset everything and push new DHCP settings to make sure all their workstations were getting the right addresses

They're on a fifty user license right now so that's fine but I did mention that they might want to upgrade to a seventy five user license in the next quarter or so actually I should check with their billing department first because I think they're going to be over the limit in a couple weeks

I'm just trying to get this sandwich finished and I keep forgetting why I even started this call they're going to be fine though I'm pretty confident in the fixes we implemented the only thing is they've got to be more careful about their documentation because I spent like an hour just trying to figure out which device was connected where

Maybe I should create a simple network map for them actually wait I have one saved in my email I'll send that over

I can't believe I'm still hungry I need to go back to that call um I mean back to that conversation I guess

persona: an indie game developer six months from launch
topic: recap of an appointment they just left (doctor, mechanic, bank, vet)
text:
Okay so I just got back from the doctor's office and I'm feeling pretty good about it actually. Dr. Martinez was really thorough, took forever with my checkup which I guess is good. She said my blood work came back normal, which is great because I've been worried about that since my last visit. She wants me to come back in three months for a follow-up, and she mentioned something about my cholesterol levels being borderline, so I'm going to have to pay more attention to what I eat. The nurse, her name's Sarah, she's been working there for like five years now and she's really nice. She took my blood pressure twice and the second time it was a little better than the first time. She told me that's good, that I should keep doing what I'm doing.

Then I went to get my car fixed at AutoCare on Fifth Street. The mechanic, his name's Tom, he's been working on my Honda for like three years now and he's pretty reliable. He said the brake pads need to be replaced and the oil needs to be changed. Total cost is six hundred and forty-seven dollars, which is a little more than I expected. But honestly I think it's worth it. My car has been making this weird squeaking noise when I brake and I was getting really worried it might be something serious. Tom said he'll have it done by Friday, so I should be good to go. I'm going to need to get a new oil filter though, but that's another expense.

I also had to go to the bank to sort out some paperwork. The branch manager, her name's Lisa, she helped me with the loan application for the new studio space. She said that it looks like we're going to get approved, which is really exciting. I was so worried about that part because I didn't want to get stuck with another loan that's too high. She said the terms are good and I can start paying it back next month. I had to fill out like twelve different forms, but at least I got it all done today. It felt good to get that paperwork finished, even though I'm going to have to go back for a few more things.

And then last but definitely not least, I took my dog Max to the vet. Dr. Peterson was really friendly, which I appreciate because I was so nervous about bringing him in. She said he's healthy, which is what I was hoping for. She gave him a new vaccination and he's up to date on everything. I'm supposed to take him back in six months for another checkup, but she said he's doing really well. He's still a bit of a handful when he gets excited, but he's growing up so fast. I think I'm going to need a bigger dog park for him.

So I think that's everything. I'm going to go make some coffee and then I'm going to start working on that new level design for the game. I feel pretty good about this whole week actually. I've been feeling pretty overwhelmed lately with the launch and all, but taking care of myself and my things is really important. I need to remember to keep doing that.

persona: a PhD student in marine biology writing their dissertation
topic: recap of a meeting that just ended
text:
Okay so anyway I just got back from Dr. Martinez's meeting and I'm really annoyed about the whole thing like I said I'm really annoyed about the whole thing and I don't know why I'm even bothering to explain this because I know you guys are probably like, "Oh yeah we all know how frustrating these meetings are" but honestly it was a complete waste of time. 

We were supposed to discuss the Pacific salmon population data from last month, but like I said it was a complete waste of time. I don't know why she had to go off on this tangent about weather patterns again because I mean, it's like, it's not even like, I know it sounds stupid but it's like we literally have three weeks of data that we've been collecting and she's still going on about how the El Niño cycle is affecting the temperature readings and I'm like, completely irrelevant to our specific research questions. 

I mean, I understand she wants to be comprehensive, but like I said we literally have three months of research that we could actually analyze and present but instead we're spending time on the weather. 

I also asked about the funding allocation for the next field season and she basically just said, "Well, we'll see what happens" and that's probably the least helpful answer you can give and I'm getting frustrated honestly. 

Oh and by the way, I did mention that we should really start planning for the tagging program since the funding comes from the National Marine Foundation but that got completely ignored. I mean I know we're all under pressure but it's like, we've been working on this for two years now and I just want to know if we're actually going to get the resources we need to finish the research. 

I'm starting to think we're just going to have to delay everything until next spring which is really not ideal and I'll probably go back to working on the statistical analysis now which at least I can actually control. 

I don't know, I just feel like we're not making any progress and it's really frustrating because like I said, I don't think I've said this before, but we're running out of time, like, I mean I know we're all under pressure but like, we've been working on this for two years now and I just want to know if we're actually going to get the resources we need to finish the research. 

I'm starting to think we're just going to have to delay everything until next spring which is really not ideal. 

I'll probably go back to working on the statistical analysis now which at least I can actually control.

persona: a farmer managing a small organic vegetable operation
topic: money and budget planning
text: So I'm sitting here in the car thinking about the budget again, you know, need to figure out how much money I'm going to have for the next season, right? The soil tests came back from Johnson's place, and it's like twelve hundred dollars, which is kind of crazy, but I guess that's what we're paying for good quality, you know? I've got to pay Mike for the tractor work next week, that's six hundred dollars, and then there's the seed order from Valley Farms, three hundred, and I'm still trying to decide if I want to buy that new compost bin, which is like two hundred and fifty dollars, right? Oh, and I need to talk to the county about the grant for the irrigation system, that's the one I applied for last fall, you know? I just need to make sure everything's lined up, because we're starting to get some serious rain, and I don't want to lose any of the crops, you know, um, I mean, I really don't want to lose any of the crops.

persona: a farmer managing a small organic vegetable operation
topic: recap of a meeting that just ended
text:
Okay so I just got back from the county extension meeting and I wanted to run through a few things with myself really quick, like I said, so the meeting was at the community center on Main Street right after the Tuesday morning farmers market, which is always the best time to catch people, you know, I mean it's like, I mean I don't know, it's just, yeah anyway there was like twelve folks there mostly from the organic growers group and I think we're supposed to have a new meeting scheduled for next week that's what they said in the notes, right?

So the thing about the soil testing, you know, I'm going to need to send samples from the north field, that's the one that's been getting a bit more rain lately, the soil map showed we're getting some runoff issues there so I want to test for nitrogen levels, which is what they said, the soil guy his name is Bob or maybe it was Joe I can't remember exactly, anyway he said we should sample around the 15th of the month, so that's actually this Friday which means I need to get out there early and not wait until the last minute like I did last year, right?

And then there's the compost situation, I know I've been putting a lot of kitchen scraps in that pile but I think we're going to need to add some more brown materials, I've been getting a good supply from the local restaurant, you know, the one down on Fifth Street they give me a good amount of their vegetable peelings that's been super helpful, I still have about three and a half cubic yards of compost in the bin so that's good to keep going.

The funding application is another thing that's on my mind, I looked at the email again, there's a new grant opportunity from the state agriculture department, the deadline is coming up fast, like in two weeks or maybe a bit more, I think I'm going to need to prepare a more detailed budget, you know, like I've been doing with the greenhouse expansion, right?

The neighbor's kid Tommy said he was interested in working with us this season, so that's been nice, he's been helping me with the planting and I think we'll need to talk about some terms, I should probably make a quick list of what he'll be doing and how much we'll be paying him, maybe $8 an hour for now that's what we agreed on right?

Oh and I just remembered I need to order the seeds again from the supplier in Oregon, the one with the greenhouse photos on their website, I think it's called Sunny Side Seeds, the order form was in my notebook, they were supposed to send me the variety packs for the early summer crops, I'm hoping they'll have them in stock.

I'm just going to go ahead and get everything together for the next meeting, I know it's going to be a good one, we're really getting this operation moving, I've been thinking about maybe expanding into some new crops like the microgreens, that's something I've been researching, the local market seems interested in more specialty items so that might be the way to go, I just need to get some more information, I think I'll call them again next week, I think they mentioned something about having a sample shipment available.

persona: a nurse who works night shifts at a regional hospital
topic: recap of a call with a customer or client
text:
Okay, so I just got off the phone with Mrs. Rodriguez from the cardiology department, you know, the one who's been complaining about her chest pain lately, right? She said she's been having these episodes three times a week now, usually around midnight, which is kind of weird because she's on the night shift, right?

Anyway, she was really frustrated because she said Dr. Martinez wasn't available and she kept getting transferred between nurses. I told her I'd check with the on-call physician, but honestly, I'm not even sure who's on duty tonight. I mean, it's Thursday, so that's probably Dr. Chen, but you know how it goes. 

She kept saying she's been feeling nauseous and dizzy too, not just the chest pain, which is concerning. I told her to keep track of when these episodes happen and maybe write down what she was doing beforehand, like if she had eaten or something. She seemed to think that might help.

She also mentioned her husband was in town visiting, so that's good, I guess. She said he'd been asking about her medication schedule, but I told her he could just call the hospital if he has questions. I mean, that's a reasonable request, right?

She seemed relieved when I said I'd look into it, which is always nice, but honestly, I'm exhausted and my head is kind of spinning, so I'm not sure if I was very helpful. I keep thinking about that patient who was supposed to be discharged yesterday but never actually went home, and now I'm worrying about whether we have a bed available for Mrs. Rodriguez.

But whatever, I'll check in with the doctor in the morning and see what we're supposed to do. Um, yeah, I think that's about it.

persona: a freelance graphic designer juggling three client projects

setting: right after the event they are talking about
text:
Okay so I just had this conversation with Sarah from TechFlow about the mobile app redesign project and I'm going to be honest I was really nervous going into it. She's been on my radar for a while and I knew this project was going to be a big one. She came in with some really specific requirements about the user interface and honestly I was worried I'd be able to meet all of them. But you know what I think I pulled it off.

We talked about the timeline and the budget and I realized I needed to be upfront about something. I've been working on three projects right now and I was starting to feel a little stretched thin but I didn't want to lose this client so I went ahead and made a decision that I'm actually pretty proud of. I told her that I could definitely handle the redesign but I needed to shift my focus to this one project first and I was going to be honest about the timeline.

She seemed really understanding which actually made me feel better. I told her that I'm going to have to prioritize my time and that I've been able to get a bit more flexible with how I structure my work especially now that I'm doing a bit more freelance work. I've been trying to streamline my process and I think it's actually working out better for me than I expected.

So I'm going to be working on this project from Monday through Friday and I'm going to be making sure that I'm not rushing anything. I don't want to compromise on quality and I know that Sarah's not just another client. She's got a really solid reputation in the industry and I want to make sure I'm representing her company properly.

I'm also going to be making sure that I'm communicating more regularly with her which is something I've been trying to do more of lately. It's actually been really helpful. I think it's important to keep people in the loop especially when there are multiple stakeholders involved.

And honestly I'm going to be using some of the new tools I've been testing out and I think it's going to save me some time. I've been learning how to use the design system that I mentioned in the last meeting and it's been really helpful for keeping things consistent across different projects. I've also been looking into some of the new software updates and I think they're going to really improve my workflow.

The other thing I'm going to be doing is making sure I'm being realistic about my deadlines. I've been trying to build in some buffer time which has been a little bit of a challenge but I think it's worth it. I don't want to be scrambling at the last minute and I want to make sure I'm delivering quality work.

So I'm going to be keeping this in mind going forward and I think it's going to help me stay organized especially with these three projects I'm working on right now. I've been doing a lot of juggling but I think I'm getting better at it. I'm definitely going to be more proactive about setting clear expectations and I'm going to make sure I'm not overcommitting myself. I think I've learned a lot about the importance of being honest and upfront with clients and it's made a huge difference in how I approach my work.

persona: a farmer managing a small organic vegetable operation
topic: reflections after a difficult conversation
text:
Hey I'm just sitting here at the computer after the whole thing with Sarah from Green Valley Markets. I can't believe how she just dismissed our organic spinach like it was some kind of joke. I mean we've been growing that stuff for three years now and it's been our best seller every season. I don't know why she's suddenly so skeptical.

She said it's not up to standard or something but when I asked for specific feedback she just started talking about some other farm in the valley. I think it's because we're not one of the big operations anymore. You know the same place that gave us all that money last year remember? She was so excited about our tomatoes and peppers and I could tell she really wanted to keep working with us.

But this time she's just acting like we're not worth the effort. I was really mad honestly but I tried to stay professional. The worst part is I know I probably shouldn't have taken the phone call in the first place. I mean I should have been on my phone the whole time but I was trying to work on some paperwork and I heard her voice. I could tell by her tone that something was wrong.

We're already running on a tight schedule and now she's going to make me find new buyers? I've been here for almost four years and we've grown so much but this is getting frustrating. I should have kept the prices the same last season I think. I mean we're doing everything right but I guess that's not good enough for some people.

We've been using the same composting system for two years now and I've seen the results. We're getting bigger yields and the quality is better than ever. The soil's been so good I actually had to call my dad for advice because I thought the vegetables were too perfect to be real. He said we were doing great and I should trust my instincts.

I just don't understand why this is happening now when we're finally seeing some real success. I thought I'd be able to focus on the business side of things but instead I'm just dealing with this constant stress. I'm going to call her tomorrow morning and try to figure out what she wants. Maybe if we could just talk we could sort this out.

I know I'm not going to be able to sleep right now but I'll get some rest eventually. Tomorrow's a new day and I have a lot to do. The new greenhouse is almost ready and we need to start planning for next season's planting. I'm going to make sure everything's perfect because I'm tired of being second-guessed. I'll call the other buyers and see what they think. I know there are more people out there who appreciate good food.

persona: a PhD student in marine biology writing their dissertation
topic: money and budget planning

text:
Okay so I need to get this budget stuff sorted out because we're running out of funds and I have to submit the final proposal by Friday. So the grant money came through but it's only 48,000 and that's not nearly enough for the research I need to do. I mean I can't just keep calling my advisor Dr. Martinez and asking for more money because he's already said no twice, and I'm pretty sure he's not happy about it.

I'm thinking I need to apply for the marine research fellowship next semester, but that's going to take me at least two weeks to complete all the paperwork, so that's probably too late. Maybe I can cut back on some of the diving equipment expenses. I did mention earlier that I need to rent a submersible for two weeks in the Pacific, but maybe I could just do the surface diving instead. That would save me around 3,000 dollars, which is nice but not enough.

Actually wait, let me check my budget spreadsheet again. I think I could probably reduce the travel costs if I take the train instead of flying. That might save me about 1,500 dollars. I also need to make sure I'm using all the funding efficiently, so I'll have to see if I can get some of the equipment I already own to count toward the research expenses. The underwater camera I bought last year, I think that could be used for this project.

I really need to get this done today though because I'm already behind schedule and I can't afford to lose any more time. I'll just have to make some difficult decisions about what I can and can't afford to do.

persona: a PhD student in marine biology writing their dissertation
topic: brainstorm of ideas for a new project
setting: in the kitchen while doing something else
text:
So I was thinking about this whole coral bleaching thing and how it's been going on for like three years now, right? I mean, it's not even really bleaching anymore, it's more like the whole ecosystem just collapsing, which is kind of terrifying because I've been studying this particular reef system off the coast of Maui for about six months now. The data I've been collecting on the temperature spikes and the pH levels and the algal blooms, it's all consistent with what we've seen in the literature, but I'm wondering if there's something unique about this specific location that's making it worse.

I keep thinking about how we could design a study that actually addresses this problem directly instead of just documenting it. Um, maybe we could create a controlled environment where we're testing different types of coral species in various combinations, and then measuring their resistance to temperature fluctuations and acidification. You know, like we did last semester with the lab experiments, but on a larger scale. I mean, the funding for that would be ridiculous, but maybe we could do a smaller pilot study first, like with maybe just six different coral colonies, and see how they respond to different stress levels.

I was actually thinking about this over breakfast this morning, which is kind of sad because I'm pretty sure I've been eating cereal for like three weeks straight now, and I'm just tired of oatmeal. I was looking at the notes from that conference I went to in San Diego last month, and they mentioned some new techniques for monitoring coral health that involve using drones and satellite imagery. I don't know, I'm not sure that's going to work for a small-scale study, but it might be something we could combine with our ground-level data.

Actually, wait, let me think about this differently. What if instead of focusing on the corals themselves, we looked at the symbiotic algae or whatever they're called, the zooxanthellae, and how they're affected by the changing conditions. There's this one paper from 2019 that suggested that certain types of algae might be more resilient than others, and if we could identify which ones, we could potentially engineer or select for more resistant strains.

I mean, I'm really exhausted today, I think I've been working too hard, and it's been raining for like two weeks straight, which is kind of depressing, but it's also good for the reef because the water temperature stays lower, but then again that's not necessarily good for the coral because they need to be warm enough to survive, but not too warm, I guess.

The thing is, I'm really tired of just documenting problems, and I want to be part of the solution, you know? I don't know, maybe I'll ask Dr. Martinez about the drone stuff, or maybe we could collaborate with some of the other marine biology labs at the university who are working on restoration projects. It's just a lot of work, and I'm not even sure I can handle all this pressure anymore. But I think this might actually be a good project, something that makes a real difference, and that's what I've been wanting to do since I started this program.

I should probably call my mom soon, she's been asking about the research progress, and I've been putting it off because I'm tired, and I can't even remember what I was doing last week. The deadlines are coming up, I'm sure, but I just don't have the energy to think about it right now. Maybe I'll write this down and then figure out the next steps tomorrow morning.

persona: a farmer managing a small organic vegetable operation
topic: coordinating schedules with other people
text:
Okay so I need to call Sarah at the farmers market um I mean I need to call Sarah at the farmers market about getting her to pick up some of the extra tomatoes we've got from the greenhouse. She mentioned last week she needed some more of those cherry varieties for her little stand there and we've got like thirty pounds or so ready to go. Then I need to check in with Miguel from the Community Supported Agriculture program. He's been asking about when we're getting ready to ship the next batch of greens and herbs. I've been thinking about that for the past few days and I think we can get the first load out Monday morning but I want to make sure the weather stays good through the weekend. I keep checking the forecast and it looks like we'll have some rain coming in on Saturday which is going to mess up our planting schedule for next week so I want to get that delivery out before the storm hits.

There's also the issue with our irrigation system that we've been putting off fixing. That's been on my mind for like two months now and I'm starting to think we're going to have to spend some money on it. The water pressure's been really inconsistent especially in the greenhouse section and it's making the plants look a little stressed. I should probably look into getting a quote from the irrigation company we used last year. That was Mr. Rodriguez right? His number was on the back of the invoice that's still sitting in my truck.

Actually speaking of our truck I should probably fill up the gas tank before we head out tomorrow to the wholesale market. We're not going to make it all the way down to the big city without a full tank and I don't want to get stuck in some rural area with a dead battery or something. Plus I've been hearing from our wholesale customers that they're really happy with how the organic carrots are coming along especially the ones we planted in the early spring.

I need to send a message to Tom about the delivery schedule for the upcoming week. He's been very reliable with his pick-up times so I should be able to set him up for the usual Friday morning delivery but I want to make sure there's no confusion about the quantities. He always wants to know the exact weight of each shipment and he's very particular about the quality standards.

I also want to call my sister Jessica about her visit next week. She's been asking about the operation so I should probably give her a quick update on what we're working on and she mentioned she'd be interested in helping with the greenhouse work on Saturday morning if the weather cooperates. That would be great actually because I've been feeling overwhelmed with everything lately and having some extra hands would help.

persona: a real-estate agent in a mid-size city
topic: recap of an appointment they just left (doctor, mechanic, bank, vet)
text: Okay so um I went to Dr. Martinez's office at three thirty which was totally ridiculous because I literally just saw him two days ago for a routine checkup and I'm like I'm like why did I schedule this appointment um like I don't even remember what I was talking about but anyway then I drove to Jackson Auto Repair on Fifth Street and they said my brakes need replacing and it's going to cost three fifty and that's just for the front ones and I'm like seriously like, what? I can't believe it, you know? persona: a freelance graphic designer juggling three client projects
topic: capturing a product or business idea before it slips away
text:
Okay so I'm sitting here at the coffee shop on Fifth Street and I'm supposed to be working on the Johnson account but I keep getting distracted by this really weird idea I had this morning about maybe creating a whole new product line for them you know like that thing I was thinking about the other day with the organic skincare line but this would be completely different so I'm just going to write this down real quick okay

So the Johnson account right it's that whole eco-friendly cleaning brand they want me to design a new website for and I've been working on it since Tuesday and it's supposed to launch next Friday but I keep thinking about this other concept this product idea you know what I'm saying the thing that was buzzing around in my head all day long and I keep getting interrupted by people asking me about this or that I'm just like stop let me think for a minute

So let's see this idea is like what if we could create these biodegradable packaging materials that are actually kind of fun to look at you know like instead of just generic white boxes or whatever what if we had these really colorful almost like little art pieces right something that people would actually want to keep and use for their own stuff after they've used the product I know I know I sound like I'm talking about something that's going to change the world or whatever

I remember talking to Sarah from the marketing team on Monday she was saying something about how people want to feel like they're making a positive impact and not just buying another thing which is true right and I was like well what if we could create this whole experience around the packaging I was actually thinking about this last night while I was lying in bed just scrolling through Instagram and seeing all these cool product photography posts and I'm just like wait what if I could take this whole idea and make it a thing not necessarily the Johnson thing but something that could maybe become a side project or even a separate company

I think I saw some kind of prototype or something on this design blog I follow I think it was called something like BioPack or maybe it was something else I can't remember exactly but the concept was like the same thing really biodegradable materials with aesthetic appeal but I'm not going to steal that idea or anything I just want to think through this one I think the way it would work is we'd have a whole series of different shapes like little hexagons or triangles that would be sort of like a puzzle right people could actually build little structures out of them or just display them on their desks or whatever

And then there's also the whole sustainability angle you know the whole point of this is that it's completely biodegradable and it would decompose in like six months or something which would be really good for the environment but also for people who want to feel like they're doing the right thing I'm just thinking that this might actually be something that could be successful not just because of the aesthetic factor but because it would solve a real problem right now people are just throwing away all these plastic containers it's ridiculous but then again we're all kind of stuck in this world where you can't really change everything at once but maybe we could start small

I just really need to get back to this project though okay I have to send those final mockups to Mr. Peterson by end of day he's really pushing for that timeline but I'm going to make sure I get this idea down somewhere like I should probably write a little sketch or something maybe even call my mom about this she always says I should trust my instincts and I've been feeling like this is a real opportunity I just don't want to lose track of it because I know it's important I know it's going to be something that people will really respond to

I also need to call the Johnson team back I've been putting it off for like three days now and I think I've got to start making some progress on that website or they're going to be really disappointed which is fine I guess but I'd rather not have to explain why I was distracted all this morning

persona: a real-estate agent in a mid-size city

Anyway, the whole time I was sitting there, I kept thinking about this concept for a mobile app that helps people find local artisans and craftspeople. I know, I know, it sounds kind of generic, but the way I see it, we're in a bit of a transition period right now where people are looking for more authentic experiences.

I mean, the Johnsons were talking about how they want their new place to have that character, you know? And I was thinking, what if we could create a platform where local woodworkers, metalworkers, even those people who do custom quilting or pottery, could actually get discovered? 

It's interesting because I just looked up the numbers - there are like 3,200 registered artisans in our city alone who aren't getting enough exposure. And the idea that I'm working on right now - it's not just about connecting them with customers, it's about building communities. 

I need to write down the specific features I had in mind because they're kind of floating around in my head right now. I know it's a bit of a long shot, but I think there's something there, something there.

persona: a PhD student in marine biology writing their dissertation
topic: todo list for the next few days
setting: in the kitchen while doing something else
text:
Okay so I need to get this dissertation done and I'm feeling really motivated right now. First thing tomorrow I'm calling Dr. Martinez about the coral bleaching data from the Pacific expedition. She said she'd have those numbers ready by Tuesday so I should check in with her then. Then I need to review the feedback from my committee meeting on Friday. I remember they wanted more statistical analysis on the sea turtle population numbers. 

I think I should start drafting the methodology chapter this week, maybe Monday or Tuesday. I need to go to the library on Thursday to get some additional sources on ocean acidification studies. That's the whole point of my research, right? Ocean acidification and its impact on marine ecosystems, particularly the kelp forests off the coast of California. 

I'm also supposed to meet with my lab partner Sarah next Friday to discuss our fieldwork schedule. She's been working on the fish population surveys and I think she's found some interesting patterns with the juvenile fish counts. I need to check my email for her latest findings, which she promised to send by Wednesday.

I should probably schedule some time to work on my presentation for the Marine Biology Association conference next month. I've been putting that off too long. Actually, I think I'll start that tomorrow morning. The conference is in Boston, so I'll need to book my flight by the end of the week. 

I also have to finish the literature review section. It's due next Monday, but I think I can get it done by Friday if I focus on it. I'm really excited about this research, and I want to make sure I do it justice. This is going to be really important for my career, so I need to stay organized and on schedule.

Um, let me see, what else do I need to do? Oh right, I need to um, I need to follow up with Dr. Martinez about the coral bleaching data, and then I need to check my email for Sarah's findings. 

Uh, I keep thinking I should start the methodology chapter now but I'm not sure if I'm ready to start that yet. I mean, I need to be careful about the statistical analysis because they wanted more detailed work on the sea turtle numbers. 

I think I'm going to start working on the presentation tomorrow morning, and then I'll need to book my flight to Boston, right? 

Actually, no, I think I'll do that by the end of the week. I don't want to wait too long. 

I also need to review the feedback from my committee meeting again because I remember they wanted more statistical analysis and I think I need to double-check that. 

Oh, and I should probably take a look at the literature review section again, make sure I'm not missing anything important. 

I'm really excited about this research and I want to make sure I do it justice. This is going to be really important for my career.

persona: a startup founder running a six-person B2B SaaS company
topic: recap of a meeting that just ended
text:
Okay so I just got back from meeting with Sarah and the team at the coffee shop on Fifth Street and we talked through the Q4 goals and I'm really excited about where we're going. So Sarah brought up this thing about the customer retention metrics and we're actually up twelve percent year-over-year which is fantastic, I mean we've been working on that churn reduction thing for like three months now and it's finally paying off.

Mike from the engineering team was super pumped about the new feature they're building for the dashboard, it's going to be live next week and I think it's going to really help with those conversion numbers. We're also planning to launch the new pricing tier tomorrow, which is going to help us capture more mid-market clients. The demo with the potential enterprise client from Johnson & Johnson went really well, they loved the security features we built in last month.

I'm also really happy with how the marketing campaign is performing, the lead conversion rate is up twenty percent since we started using those new email templates. We need to make sure we have enough bandwidth for all these new clients though, I think we might need to hire two more people next month. I'll follow up with the recruiting team after lunch. Anyway, I'm feeling really good about where we're at, we're hitting all our targets, and I think we're going to crush Q4. The team's really motivated and I can feel this energy, it's really exciting. I just want to make sure we keep this momentum going and continue to build on what we've accomplished so far. This is the kind of progress that makes all the long hours worth it.

persona: an IT consultant who travels to client sites most weeks
topic: preparing for an upcoming interview or presentation
text:
Okay so I'm walking to the client site today and I'm feeling really energized because I've been working on this presentation for the Johnson account and I think it's finally coming together. The thing is, I've been traveling a lot lately, you know, three different client sites last week, which is exhausting but also really good for building my confidence. I was in Boston Tuesday and then I had to fly to Denver Thursday and I'm just so glad I don't have to do the trip back to Boston again for another month or something like that.

But speaking of the Johnson account, I've been thinking about how I want to structure this presentation and honestly I think I should go with the new dashboard format. That's what we discussed last time and I've been practicing with the numbers and everything. The quarterly report is looking really solid. Actually, let me check my phone for just a second but I'm not going to do that, I'll just keep walking because I'm feeling good about this.

I think I should start with the financial overview, maybe do a quick recap of the last quarter and then dive into the specific metrics that matter. I keep thinking about how we should probably emphasize the cost savings they've achieved this quarter, which is about twelve percent more than projected. That's going to be the big talking point. I've got the slides ready, they're on my laptop, and I've even got the backup USB drive in my bag, just in case. The client's going to be in the conference room at three o'clock, so I want to make sure I'm there at two thirty, or maybe even earlier, just to set up.

I'm also going to make sure I've got a good story to tell. I know that the numbers are important but I also need to paint a picture of how we've helped them, not just show them the spreadsheets. Maybe I should talk about the specific improvements in their workflow, the efficiency gains, the customer satisfaction ratings. The Johnson account is actually one of the most important ones for us right now. I've been working with Sarah on that for like eight months now and she's really been pushing for this presentation, so I don't want to mess it up.

I should probably review the feedback from last time, that's important. I mean, they said they liked the analysis but they wanted more detailed examples of the implementation process. I've got that information ready. I'm also going to make sure I have the right materials, like the detailed process documentation, the user training materials. They've requested more information about the user onboarding process, which I think is great because that shows they're really engaged, not just passing by.

I think I'm going to be ready for this. I'm feeling confident about the preparation, I've got all the technical details and I've even done some practice runs with my colleague Tom. He's been helping me with the visual elements and I think he's right, the charts and graphs are going to work really well. I'm just walking to this meeting but I feel like I'm actually running, like I'm moving towards something important and I'm excited about it. It's going to be a good day, I can feel it.

persona: a real-estate agent in a mid-size city
topic: notes on an article they just read or a video they just watched
text:
Okay so I just finished watching this video about the new zoning changes in downtown Portland and I'm totally excited about this. So like, the city council actually passed these new regulations last Tuesday and I'm telling you, this is going to be huge for our market here in the Pearl District.

You know what I was thinking, the article said that the new residential density rules mean we're looking at about thirty percent more development potential in the core area. That's like, that's incredible. And it's not just the numbers, it's the timing. I mean, the video mentioned that the new development is going to start happening in like six months, right? So that's perfect timing for us, because we've got this new listing coming up on Tuesday. That's the one I was telling you about with the two-story condo on Fifth Street.

And the thing is, the article also talked about how the city is going to invest in infrastructure, right? Like the new subway access and all those improvements to the street grid. That's going to make this area even more attractive, especially for young professionals. I was thinking about that house in the Eastside area, the one that's been on the market for over a month now. I bet if I had more time to really work on the presentation, I could get it sold for way more than the current asking price.

I mean, it's not just about the numbers, though we do need those. It's about positioning ourselves correctly. You know, the real estate market is shifting now, and I'm seeing it everywhere. Like, my client Sarah from the business district, she was actually asking about some of the new developments along the riverfront. That's a huge area that's going to see some serious investment over the next couple years.

The video I watched also mentioned that there are going to be more mixed-use developments, which means the market is evolving. I'm thinking that's really good news for us, because we've been pushing for a more diverse portfolio. You know, the new listing that's coming up, I'm going to need to get some better photos, maybe even do some virtual walkthroughs for people who can't make it in person.

The thing that really made me excited was the part about how they're encouraging more sustainable building practices. That's going to be huge. People are becoming more environmentally conscious, and that's going to drive up demand for properties with green features. I should probably update my marketing materials to highlight those aspects, because I think that's what buyers are looking for now.

And the timing, like I said, it's perfect. I'm thinking that if I can get the word out about these new developments and the infrastructure improvements, we're going to have a busy spring. I'm actually going to call the developer who's working on the new project on the corner of First and Pine. I need to get some details about the upcoming units, because that's the kind of property that's going to really attract the right kind of buyers.

I'm just really energized about this, you know? It's not often that we get to be part of something this big, and I feel like we're positioned to take advantage of it. The real estate market is definitely changing, and we're going to be right there with it. I need to start putting together some presentations and start reaching out to our existing clients to let them know about these opportunities. I'm going to make sure I'm ready for whatever comes next, because this is going to be a big moment for our business.

persona: an indie game developer six months from launch
topic: recap of a call with a customer or client
setting: in the kitchen while doing something else
text:
Okay, so I just got back from this call with Sarah from Cloud9 Studios and man I'm exhausted. She was really upset about the build issues we had last week, you know, the one with the save file corruption that crashed everyone's progress. Anyway, she's been working with us for like three months now, so I can't just ignore her concerns.

She basically said we need to get the next patch out by Friday or she's going to have to put the whole project on hold. I told her we're already working on it but she was pretty firm about the timeline. Also, she mentioned that some of the other developers who are working with them are starting to question whether our team is actually delivering what we promised. She was pretty direct about the fact that they're getting pressure from their investors.

I'm just sitting here in my kitchen, about to make some coffee, but I can't stop thinking about how we're going to pull this off. I mean, the original launch was supposed to be this huge success, but now we're just scrambling to keep everyone happy. I should probably call the team and see if we can get something together for tomorrow morning. Maybe if we push the developers a bit harder they can get the patch done on time.

Honestly, I don't know how much longer I can keep this up. It's been six months since launch, and I feel like we're drowning in technical debt and client requests. I just want to finish this thing and move on to something else. Um, yeah, that's pretty much it. I'm going to grab some coffee now and then I'll call the team. Uh, I hope we can figure this out because I'm really worried about the situation. You know, I just really hope we can get this patch out on time, and I'm not sure how we're going to do it.

persona: a freelance graphic designer juggling three client projects
topic: planning a home repair or improvement
text:
Okay, so I'm walking to the hardware store right now and I keep thinking about this whole home repair thing I'm supposed to be working on. I need to get some new tiles for the bathroom floor, you know, the ones with the blue and green pattern that I picked out last week.

Wait, let me check my notes here. The contractor said he'd be here tomorrow at nine, but I'm pretty sure he's going to be late. That's the third time this month, and honestly I'm starting to wonder if he's just not taking me seriously. I mean, I've been pretty clear about what I want, but it's like he just doesn't get it.

I'm walking past the coffee shop on Fifth Street and I keep thinking about this client project I have going with Sarah from marketing. She wants these new business cards and I'm like, Sarah, I already told you I'm swamped with three different client projects right now, but she keeps sending me these emails with these crazy deadlines that are just completely unreasonable. I'm not even sure I'm going to be able to finish everything on time.

The tiles are supposed to be 12 by 12 inches, and I need about 45 square feet of them, but I'm not going to lie, I'm starting to think that's going to be way too much. I probably should have just stuck with the plain white ones I saw at the store last time. That would have been easier, but no, I had to go with the pattern and now I'm worried I'm going to end up with a weird mismatch.

I'm walking past the old building on Maple Avenue and I keep thinking about the other client project, the one with the restaurant owner. His name is David, and he wants these new menus for his place, and I'm like, David, we've been talking about this for weeks now. He wants everything to be perfect, but he's also not really giving me the details I need.

I've got to get back to the drawing board on that one. I need to know what kind of font he wants, what the color scheme is, and whether he wants any pictures or not. He just keeps saying he wants it to look "professional" and "modern" but that's like saying he wants his car to be "fast" and "reliable." It's not specific enough.

The hardware store is coming up, and I'm going to have to call my mom to see if she can help me figure out how to measure properly. I'm not good at that kind of math, and I don't want to make a mistake. I mean, I've already ordered the wrong size tiles once before, and I don't want to go through that again.

I'm almost there now. I need to get the exact measurements, and I also need to make sure I'm getting the right kind of grout. I know it's not just grout, it's like grout with different properties and I have to make sure I'm getting the right one for the bathroom.

I've got to get these projects done, but I'm just so tired of feeling like I'm always behind. I'm going to have to work late tonight, probably. I should have just said no to some of these projects, but now I'm committed and I can't back out. It's just going to be a long week.

persona: a startup founder running a six-person B2B SaaS company
topic: todo list for the next few days
setting: right after the event they are talking about
text:
Okay so I just got back from the board meeting and I am so irritated right now. I need to get all this stuff organized because we're supposed to be launching the new dashboard feature next month and I can't believe what happened. First of all, Sarah from marketing called me at like 11 PM last night to tell me that the campaign we were supposed to launch this week is completely dead. I don't know what happened with the ad spend or whatever but they're saying it's because of some weird technical issue that they didn't even tell us about. I'm going to have to call her back in the morning and get an actual explanation.

Then there's the whole thing with the data migration. You know how I told you about the client data being moved to the new system? Well, apparently we're missing about 15% of the records from the old database and I'm pretty sure that's because Mike made a mistake with the script. I know he's been working late hours but come on, we can't have data loss right before the big launch. I'm going to have to sit down with him and make sure he understands what happened.

I also need to call the legal team about the compliance updates we talked about. They said they would have the document ready by Thursday but I haven't heard anything yet and I'm running out of time. The last thing we need is to get hit with a compliance issue at this point.

And then there's the quarterly review with our investors. I've been meaning to schedule that for a couple weeks now and I completely forgot until just now. We need to be prepared to show them the progress on the enterprise clients and the revenue projections. That's going to take some serious work to get everything together.

I also need to call the cloud provider about the infrastructure upgrade. They said they'd have everything ready by next Friday but I haven't been able to reach anyone for the past two days. This is so frustrating because we're supposed to be scaling up the server capacity.

Then I want to look at the competitor analysis I've been meaning to do. They're launching a new feature that might actually impact our market position. I need to get the full report and start thinking about our response strategy.

Oh, and I need to review the latest user feedback from the customer portal. There are some major issues that were reported last week that need to be addressed immediately. We can't let these things pile up.

I should probably also update the project timeline in Asana. I'm just going to go through each task and make sure everything is still on track. The client onboarding process needs to be streamlined, and we need to make sure we're delivering the quality we promised to our existing customers.

I need to check my email for the quarterly reports that were due yesterday. I've been so busy with the board meeting and other urgent issues that I completely forgot. I really need to get better about following up on these things.

And I have to remember to call my mom back. She's been trying to reach me about the family thing for weeks now and I just haven't had time. I'll do that later.

I think I need to make a priority list for tomorrow. The most urgent items are definitely the client data issue, the legal documents, and the investor review. The rest can wait. I should probably have a meeting with the team to go over all the important updates and make sure everyone's on the same page.

This week has been absolute chaos and I'm just really tired of constantly playing catch up.

persona: a parent of two who does most of the household cooking
topic: reflections after a difficult conversation
setting: in the kitchen while doing something else
text: I'm making dinner right now and I just got back from talking to my mom which was really hard. She called and said she thinks we should move back to Minnesota and I just, I can't even. I mean we've been here for five years now and Sarah's starting third grade and I've got this job at the hospital. I know she's worried about my dad but this whole thing about the family situation it's just so much. I'm trying to not let it show but I'm also really worried about Sarah. She's been acting so quiet lately like when I ask her about school she just says "fine." I think she's picking up on my stress which is probably not great. I need to talk to her about the situation but I don't know how. Maybe I should just call her teacher and see what's going on. I can't just keep going like this. I'm also wondering if maybe I should look into moving out of our apartment or if I'm just being dramatic. I'm really tired honestly. I've been cooking for the last hour and I'm not even sure if I'm making the right thing. Maybe I should just order pizza. I don't know I just need to process everything. It's like when I was pregnant with her I remember thinking I'd never feel so overwhelmed. I don't want to be that person the one who can't handle things. But I'm trying really. I should probably take a break from this whole conversation about the family or at least call back later. I just need a moment to breathe. I'm just really tired of feeling like I'm failing everyone.

persona: a PhD student in marine biology writing their dissertation
topic: notes from a course, workshop, or tutorial they are taking
setting: right after the event they are talking about
text:
Okay so I'm just going to quickly jot this down because I'm running late for the next session and I need to get these notes organized. So today was the coral reef monitoring workshop at the Marine Science Institute and Dr. Martinez was leading it and she was actually really good, I mean I thought she'd be boring but she's actually pretty engaging. The workshop started at ten thirty and I had to rush over from my lab because I was already ten minutes late and there was this guy in front of me in the elevator who was just talking on his phone for like ten minutes about his vacation in Bali which was just so annoying.

So the first part was about data collection protocols, which I think is going to be super useful for my own research since I'm studying the reef near the university campus. Dr. Martinez showed us these specific forms and I took pictures of them because I don't think I'll remember the exact format and she said that we need to be really careful about documenting everything because it's the data quality that's going to make or break the whole project. I got notes on the measurement techniques and I think there was something about using the underwater GPS units and the way they need to be calibrated.

She mentioned that we're supposed to record the temperature every thirty minutes and I need to make sure I get the right equipment for that because I'm going to be doing monitoring in the summer when it's really hot. Actually wait, I need to double check my field notes from last month when I did the initial survey. I think I recorded something about the average temperature but I can't remember if it was in Celsius or Fahrenheit and I definitely need to be consistent.

There was also some discussion about the different species we need to identify and she said we need to be very careful with the parrotfish because there are so many similar ones and I had a hard time telling the difference between the red and yellow ones. But she showed us these reference cards and I took pictures of those too. We went outside to do a practical exercise and I got a bit distracted because I kept thinking about how I need to finish my literature review and I'm behind on everything.

The main focus was on the transect method and she said we need to follow exactly the same procedure each time or the data won't be comparable. She demonstrated the technique and I think she said we should use a 25 meter by 5 meter area and I have a notebook with the exact measurements in there but I also need to get the actual measuring tape that's calibrated properly. I need to make sure I have that ready before I go out to the field tomorrow.

I also learned about the importance of weather conditions and the way they affect reef health. I wrote down some numbers about the pH levels and the salinity measurements from the previous year and I need to compare that to what I'm getting now. And I'm going to have to do some calculations with the data we're collecting and I'm hoping that my statistics class from last semester will help me with that.

I think the most important thing I need to remember is that the monitoring has to be done consistently and regularly, and I need to make sure I'm not going to miss any of the scheduled sessions because I'm already behind schedule with my own research and I don't want to fall even further behind.

Maybe I should just start small and see how the coffee grounds go first. I was going to write it down but I've got this whole thing about the greenhouse being too hot and needing more ventilation. I keep thinking about this new tractor part I ordered from the supplier in town, the one with the serial number 435-289, I think it was. The guy said it would come in by the end of next week. I was actually supposed to be checking the water pressure on the drip lines today but I was distracted by the neighbor's dog that's been barking all morning. It's the golden retriever that belongs to the new family down the road. Anyway, I'll try the compost method tomorrow morning before the sun gets too hot.

persona: an indie game developer six months from launch
topic: a note to their future self about a decision they just made and why
text:
Okay so I just made this huge decision that I'm not sure was the right one but here I am at like 11:45 PM writing this because I need to get it out of my head before I fall asleep and then I'll be even more worried about it tomorrow. So I'm talking about the whole thing with the art director you know Sarah Chen right? She's been working with us for like six months now and she's been absolutely incredible with the pixel art and everything's been going great but then yesterday you know I got this email from her and I'm just reading it now and it's like she's basically saying she's leaving and she's going to work at this other indie studio you know Pixel Dreams and she says that she's just not feeling challenged anymore and I don't know that's a little um I don't know I think that's kind of a cop out but okay we can deal with that.

The thing is she's got this thing you know she's been doing this freelance thing for like two years now and she's got this amazing portfolio and honestly she's been the reason why our game looks so good and the thing is I've been trying to convince her to join us full time but she kept saying no she wants to keep being freelance and I keep thinking well we can offer her more money or we can give her a better contract or we can but you know what I don't think she was ever really serious about it. So anyway she's sending me her resignation notice and she says she's been thinking about this for three weeks now and it's just kind of like okay I guess.

But here's the thing the thing that I'm mad about is I had this meeting scheduled with her for today and I'm going to be honest I was kind of hoping that she'd change her mind. I mean we've got a deadline and we're supposed to be launching on the 15th and she's been the one who's been taking care of all the art assets and now I'm going to have to find someone else and I don't even know if I can find someone else who's as good as she is and she's not even a huge name but she's been just perfect for this project.

So I'm going to have to go through the whole process of hiring a new person and that's going to take time and I don't know I'm going to have to go through interviews and all of that and I'm just frustrated because I feel like this is happening at the worst possible time and I'm going to have to go through the whole thing and I'm not even sure I'll be able to find someone who's going to be as good as she was. I'm just going to have to start from scratch and I don't know I'm probably going to be thinking about this all night now but I'm going to have to get some sleep eventually or I'm going to be completely useless tomorrow and I know she's going to be sad about it and I'm going to be sad about it but I think we both know that it's the right thing to do you know? I just wish it wasn't so hard.
